title: Phase transition in African research ecosystems: identifying the critical density for self-sustaining clinical trial capacity
primary_estimand: Critical per-capita trial density at which research ecosystems transition from dependent to self-sustaining
certainty: moderate (registry data with proxy measures for sovereignty and diversity)
body: Is there a critical per-capita trial density at which African research ecosystems transition from dependent to self-sustaining? We queried ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for 20 African countries across four dimensions: total trials, condition diversity across 12 disease categories, local sponsorship proxy, and Phase 1 trial presence, computing Shannon entropy for portfolio diversity and a composite sovereignty score. Countries above a critical density threshold exhibit qualitatively different ecosystem properties: higher local sponsorship, broader condition portfolios, and Phase 1 capability, consistent with a phase-transition model from statistical physics. Rwanda exemplifies the supercritical state with diverse local-sponsored research, while Chad remains subcritical with narrow foreign-led trials. The majority of African countries and population remain below the critical threshold in dependent or pre-critical states. These findings argue against diffuse funding and for concentrated sequential investment targeting countries nearest the transition point, where focused capacity-building can trigger self-sustaining research dynamics analogous to ecological carrying capacity.
ai_transparency: LLM used for drafting; author reviewed and takes responsibility for final content.
